dimension_explanations.alignment: As an advocacy-focused organization, the cause urgency is high, yet the low alignment score reflects a potential disconnect with standard donor preferences or specific Muslim community giving patterns [1]. The organization accepts general charitable giving through sadaqah [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization achieves a direct impact by dedicating its entire budget to programs such as National Open Mosque Day and diplomatic initiatives [1][2]. While financial efficiency is high, the lack of third-party impact evaluations contributes to a medium data confidence level [1].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize American Muslim civic engagement and want to fund a lean, high-growth umbrella organization that coordinates national advocacy.
donor_motivations: Want to support a unified diplomatic voice for American Muslims
Value extremely low overhead and high program-spending ratios
Interested in funding national-level summits and leadership development
giving_considerations: Impact is measured through policy influence and coordination rather than individual aid
The organization is still maturing its transparency and reporting standards

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects a <cite id="20">documented theory of change</cite> but a lack of third-party evaluations or longitudinal outcome tracking.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: USCMO creates impact by <cite id="1">facilitating collaboration</cite> among member groups to amplify the Muslim voice in <cite id="10">national policy discussions</cite>.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence in advocacy helps donors understand how legislative engagement translates into tangible community protections.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="4">56.8% growth rate</cite> and a <cite id="21">$530,406 funding gap</cite> identified in its niche, the org has significant capacity to scale.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="5">98.6% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core programs and services, far exceeding the <cite id="9">peer program ratio median of 85.3%</cite>.
USCMO reports a <cite id="6">fundraising cost of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that nearly every dollar donated goes toward its mission.
The charity has demonstrated rapid expansion with a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 56.8%</cite>, indicating increasing community support.

strengths_deep_dive: USCMO's primary strength lies in its role as a central hub for American Muslim advocacy. By organizing the <cite id="10">National Advocacy Day</cite>, the council provides a platform for community members to engage directly with policymakers. Financially, the organization maintains a <cite id="11">working capital of 3.4 months</cite>, providing a resilient buffer for its operations. This financial stability is supported by a <cite id="12">board where 75% of members are independent</cite>, ensuring oversight and accountability in its governance structures.
rich_narrative.summary: The US Council of Muslim Organizations (USCMO) operates as a national umbrella body dedicated to <cite id="1">coordinating educational and diplomatic initiatives</cite> for the American Muslim community. By facilitating collaboration among its member organizations, USCMO serves as a unified voice in <cite id="2">national advocacy and leadership summits</cite>. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $632,652</cite>, representing a significant <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 56.8%</cite>. A defining characteristic of the organization's financial model is its extreme leaness; it currently <cite id="5">allocates 1.0% of expenses directly to its programs</cite>. Furthermore, USCMO demonstrates high operational efficiency by maintaining a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>. Its programmatic reach includes high-visibility events such as <cite id="7">National Open Mosque Day</cite> and the <cite id="8">Annual Ramadan Iftar</cite>, which aim to bridge gaps between the Muslim community and the broader American public through diplomatic outreach.
